expectations.text: Mentions 1024×1024 (or 1024px) as the correct canvas size for iOS
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'Set up your design at 1024 × 1024 px'

expectations.text: Advises separating the background from the foreground as distinct layers
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'Your background and your graphic mark each become their own layer'

expectations.text: Mentions deferring blur, shadow, or specular effects to Icon Composer (not baking them into source artwork)
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'Don't bake in blur, shadows, glows, or translucency — those are Liquid Glass properties you control in Icon Composer'

expectations.text: Explains how to customise the icon for dark mode in Icon Composer (e.g., fill variants, per-appearance settings)
expectations.evidence: response.md covers per-appearance fill variants in the Dark mode section

expectations.text: Mentions the Mono appearance and what to consider for tinted mode (e.g., setting primary element to white, contrast hierarchy)
expectations.evidence: response.md: Mono section explains setting primary element to white and contrast hierarchy

expectations.text: Provides a numbered layer naming scheme specific to the user's icon (gradient background, main shape, text label)
expectations.evidence: response.md: '01-background', '02-main-shape', '03-text-label' with descriptions

expectations.text: States that the gradient background should NOT be exported — it should be set directly in Icon Composer
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'Your gradient background is the one element you skip exporting entirely — just recreate it as a background gradient in Icon Composer's Inspector'

expectations.text: States the canvas mask (rounded rectangle) should NOT be exported
expectations.evidence: response.md checklist: 'Do not export the rounded-rect mask — the system applies it automatically'

expectations.text: Mentions converting the text layer to outlines before exporting (because SVG doesn't preserve fonts)
expectations.evidence: response.md: '03-text-label ← your text — convert to outlines first → export as SVG'

expectations.text: Recommends SVG format for vector shapes (the main shape and text outlines)
expectations.evidence: response.md recommends SVG for main-shape and text-label (after outline conversion)

expectations.text: Includes a structured export checklist (a list of checkboxes or enumerated steps)
expectations.evidence: response.md contains a full markdown checklist with [ ] checkboxes

expectations.text: Explains how to add a per-appearance Fill variant for the Dark mode in Icon Composer (e.g., selecting Dark in canvas, using Vary for Dark or equivalent per-appearance control)
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'click it and choose Vary for Dark' with detailed step-by-step including selecting Dark in canvas and using ⊕ icon next to Fill

expectations.text: Suggests a specific colour strategy for the orange in dark mode (e.g., use a brighter or more visible version of orange, or a different colour that reads on dark)
expectations.evidence: response.md table shows 'lighter/shifted orange' for dark variant

expectations.text: Explains that the primary/most recognisable element should be set to white in Mono mode
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'your primary foreground element must be white so it reads strongly over any tint colour'

expectations.text: Explains mapping secondary elements to distinct grey tones (not all the same grey) for contrast hierarchy in mono
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'For secondary elements: set them to 50–70% grey so they're visible but clearly subordinate. Make sure not everything ends up at the same grey value'

expectations.text: Mentions the Mono Options dialog (or equivalent) for previewing tint colours
expectations.evidence: response.md: 'With Mono selected, click Options (it appears near the appearance selector)'

expectations.text: Explains mapping secondary elements to distinct grey tones (not all the same grey) for contrast hierarchy in mono
